Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand what a process variant is From the prompt a process variant is the ordered sequence of activities for a single case sorted by timestamp So for each case_id I need to get the activities in the order they happened

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The first part of the task is to construct a query that determines the sequence of activities per case_id In SQL especially in DuckDB how do I get the ordered sequence as a single string or array I remember that DuckDB has the LIST aggregate function which can collect values in order So for each case_id I can group by case_id order by timestamp and then use LIST activity to get the sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But DuckDBs LIST function returns an array which is order preserving So using LIST activity ORDER BY timestamp within each case_id group would give the sequence as an array

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So step 1 For each case_id create a list of activities ordered by timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then step 2 Group cases by this activity sequence to count how many cases have each variant

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Then the variants are grouped and counted with SELECT variant COUNT star AS frequency FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant ORDER BY frequency DESC LIMIT K

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: So the steps are 1 For each case_id compute the variant activity sequence as a list 2 Count the frequency of each variant 3 Find the top K variants highest frequencies 4 Get all case_ids that have variants in the top K 5 Select all events from the original event_log where case_id is in that set

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So in SQL WITH case_variants AS SELECT case_id LIST activity ORDER BY timestamp AS variant FROM event_log GROUP BY case_id variant_counts AS SELECT variant COUNT star AS cnt FROM case_variants GROUP BY variant ORDER BY cnt DESC LIMIT K top_variant_cases AS SELECT case_id FROM case_variants WHERE variant IN SELECT variant FROM variant_counts SELECT star FROM event_log WHERE case_id IN SELECT case_id FROM top_variant_cases

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Now checking if this meets all the requirements Correctly extract the ordered sequence of activities per case Using LIST activity ORDER BY timestamp within GROUP BY case_id does that Aggregate and count cases per variant The variant_frequencies CTE groups by variant and counts Filter down to the cases belonging to the top K variants The WHERE clause in the final query selects case_ids whose variant is in the top K variants from variant_frequencies Return the corresponding events The final SELECT star FROM event_log with the WHERE clause

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Testing with an example Suppose K equals 2 Suppose we have cases Case 1 A to B to C Case 2 A to B to C Case 3 X to Y Case 4 X to Y Case 5 A to B Then the variants are A B C with 2 cases X Y with 2 cases A B with 1 case So top 2 variants are the first two each with frequency 2 Then the final query should return all events from case_ids 1 2 3 4 Yes

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Another example if K equals 1 then only the first variant is selected

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The final query selects all events from event_log where case_id is in the set of case_ids whose variant is in the top K variants
